reason: The remediation modifies the /boot entry in /etc/fstab to add the nosuid mount option. Changing boot partition mount options can prevent the system from booting if the fstab syntax is incorrect or if the option interferes with bootloader requirements. Because it directly touches a critical filesystem used during early boot, the risk of rendering the system unbootable is higher than typical configuration changes.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Incorrect fstab syntax could cause boot failure; Adding mount option to boot partition may affect bootloader behavior; Requires a reboot to take effect, exposing a window where the system may not start
